nodes.description: Bluish sclerae are part of the original clinical description of OI type VII.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type VII: an autosomal recessive form of brittle bone disease.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype is moderate to severe, characterized by fractures at birth, bluish sclerae, early deformity of the lower extremities, coxa vara, and osteopenia.

nodes.description: CRTAP is a component of the prolyl 3-hydroxylation complex that modifies Pro986 in the alpha1(I) chain of type I collagen. Loss of this modification delays collagen folding, causing overmodification of the helix and altered collagen structure and cross-linking.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP is required for prolyl 3- hydroxylation and mutations cause recessive osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: CRTAP can form a complex with P3H1 and cyclophilin B (CYPB), and Crtap-/- bone and cartilage collagens show decreased prolyl 3-hydroxylation. Moreover, mutant collagen shows evidence of overmodification, and collagen fibrils in mutant skin have increased diameter consistent with altered fibrillogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP and LEPRE1 mutations in recessive osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: Two proteins, cartilage-associated protein (CRTAP) and prolyl-3-hydroxylase-1 (P3H1, encoded by the LEPRE1 gene) form a complex that performs the hydroxylation and brings the prolyl cis-trans isomerase cyclophilin-B (CYPB) to the unfolded collagen.

nodes.description: Some severe CRTAP variants can generate unstable CRTAP isoforms that cause loss of proline hydroxylation, type I collagen aggregation, autophagy, and cellular senescence. This captures a severe/lethal variant mechanism rather than a uniform mechanism for every CRTAP allele.
nodes.id: Collagen Proteotoxicity in Severe CRTAP Variants

evidence.reference_title: Deep intronic mutation in CRTAP results in unstable isoforms of the protein to induce type I collagen aggregation in a lethal type of osteogenesis imperfecta type VII.
evidence.snippet: Both mutant isoforms of CRTAP are unstable due to the presence of a unique 'GWxxI' degron, which finally leads to loss of proline hydroxylation and aggregation of type I collagen. Although type I collagen aggregates undergo autophagy, the overall proteotoxicity resulted in death of the proband cells by senescence.

nodes.description: Abnormal angle of the femoral neck contributing to hip dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type VII: an autosomal recessive form of brittle bone disease.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype is moderate to severe, characterized by fractures at birth, bluish sclerae, early deformity of the lower extremities, coxa vara, and osteopenia.

nodes.description: Impaired growth is reported in childhood survivors and contributes to short stature in more severely affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP deficiency leads to abnormally high bone matrix mineralization in a murine model and in children with osteogenesis imperfecta type VII.
evidence.snippet: patients have fractures at birth, deformities of the lower extremities and impaired growth

nodes.description: High myopia has been reported in an adult with CRTAP-related OI, suggesting that ocular involvement can occur outside the skeleton.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP-Related Osteogenesis Imperfecta: Clinical Variability and a Potential Founder Variant in CRTAP.
evidence.snippet: Interestingly, 1 patient presented with high myopia and bilateral retinal detachment, which have not been previously reported in OI type VII.

nodes.description: Reduced bone mineral density ranges from osteopenia in the founding cohort to extremely low bone mineral density in more severe cases.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type VII: an autosomal recessive form of brittle bone disease.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype is moderate to severe, characterized by fractures at birth, bluish sclerae, early deformity of the lower extremities, coxa vara, and osteopenia.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP and LEPRE1 mutations in recessive osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: Infants in both groups had multiple fractures, decreased bone modeling (affecting especially the femurs), and extremely low bone mineral density.

nodes.description: CRTAP deficiency in patient osteoblasts reduces CRTAP expression and prolyl 3-hydroxylation of type I collagen, with reduced osteoblast numbers, diminished osteoid volume, and markedly impaired active bone formation.
nodes.id: Osteoblast Dysfunction and Impaired Bone Formation

evidence.reference_title: Genetic Analysis, Phenotypic Spectrum and Functional Study of Rare Osteogenesis Imperfecta Caused by CRTAP Variants.
evidence.snippet: In a patient with c.621 + 1G > A and c.1153-3C > G mutations in CRTAP, the mRNA and protein levels of CRTAP in osteoblasts were significantly decreased and the osteoid volume and osteoblast numbers were markedly reduced compared with those in the normal control individual. This was simultaneously accompanied by significantly reduced prolyl 3-hydroxylation at Pro986 in the α1 chain of type I collagen and invisible active bone formation in bone.

nodes.description: Severe osteoporosis is part of the more severe CRTAP-related OI type VII phenotype spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic Analysis, Phenotypic Spectrum and Functional Study of Rare Osteogenesis Imperfecta Caused by CRTAP Variants.
evidence.snippet: Patients with OI type VII had early-onset recurrent fractures, severe osteoporosis, and bone deformities.

nodes.description: Platybasia has been reported as part of the craniofacial phenotype in severe childhood OI type VII.

evidence.reference_title: Craniofacial and dental phenotype of two girls with osteogenesis imperfecta due to mutations in CRTAP.
evidence.snippet: Cone-beam computed tomography showed occipital bossing, platybasia and wormian bones.

nodes.description: Crtap knockout mouse data support a primary lung contribution in recessive OI, with altered collagen post-translational modification in lung fibroblasts, parenchymal changes, and impaired respiratory mechanics. This supports respiratory surveillance while remaining model-organism evidence.

evidence.reference_title: Respiratory defects in the CrtapKO mouse model of osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: Here, we analyzed the lung phenotype of Crtap knockout (KO) mice, a mouse model of recessive OI. While we confirm changes in the lung parenchyma that are reminiscent of emphysema, we show that CrtapKO lung fibroblasts synthesize type I collagen with altered posttranslation modifications consistent with those observed in bone and skin.

nodes.description: Bone fragility typically manifests with recurrent fractures beginning prenatally, at birth, or in early childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic Analysis, Phenotypic Spectrum and Functional Study of Rare Osteogenesis Imperfecta Caused by CRTAP Variants.
evidence.snippet: Patients with OI type VII had early-onset recurrent fractures, severe osteoporosis, and bone deformities.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type VII: an autosomal recessive form of brittle bone disease.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype is moderate to severe, characterized by fractures at birth, bluish sclerae, early deformity of the lower extremities, coxa vara, and osteopenia.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP-Related Osteogenesis Imperfecta: Clinical Variability and a Potential Founder Variant in CRTAP.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype varied extensively in terms of severity and clinical features, ranging from moderate (type IV) to severe (type III) and including cases with prenatal fractures as well as one case with a low number of fractures and no prenatal fractures.

nodes.description: Bilateral retinal detachment has been reported in an adult with CRTAP-related OI type VII.

evidence.reference_title: CRTAP-Related Osteogenesis Imperfecta: Clinical Variability and a Potential Founder Variant in CRTAP.
evidence.snippet: Interestingly, 1 patient presented with high myopia and bilateral retinal detachment, which have not been previously reported in OI type VII.

nodes.description: Characteristic shortening of the proximal limb segments (humerus, femur), which helps distinguish recessive OI from dominant forms.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type VII: an autosomal recessive form of brittle bone disease.
evidence.snippet: Rhizomelia is a prominent clinical feature.

nodes.description: Long-bone and other skeletal deformities arise early and contribute to clinical severity.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic Analysis, Phenotypic Spectrum and Functional Study of Rare Osteogenesis Imperfecta Caused by CRTAP Variants.
evidence.snippet: Patients with OI type VII had early-onset recurrent fractures, severe osteoporosis, and bone deformities.

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type VII: an autosomal recessive form of brittle bone disease.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype is moderate to severe, characterized by fractures at birth, bluish sclerae, early deformity of the lower extremities, coxa vara, and osteopenia.

nodes.description: Wormian bones have been reported in severe craniofacial involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Craniofacial and dental phenotype of two girls with osteogenesis imperfecta due to mutations in CRTAP.
evidence.snippet: Cone-beam computed tomography showed occipital bossing, platybasia and wormian bones.
